channel.title: Evaluations on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Evaluations on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: commandline: The durable asset is the loop you own. OpenEnv is its protocol.
item.description:
The article argues that the durable asset in agentic AI is the learning loop, not the rented model, and presents OpenEnv as the protocol for reinforcement learning environments. It positions OpenEnv around owning and standardizing the environment/evaluation loop for recursive self-improvement.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Evaluations with Intelligent Trace Sampling
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry observability now supports intelligent trace filtering and sampling for evaluations in public preview. It evaluates a representative subset of production traces instead of all traces.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Managed virtual network for evaluations in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now generally supports managed virtual networks for evaluations. This lets cloud evaluation jobs reach models and agents protected by private endpoints using a Foundry-managed VNET for outbound connectivity.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: Build agents you can trust across any framework with open evals and a control standard
item.description:
Microsoft describes a set of tools for building AI agents with open evaluations, portable runtime controls, observability, and security workflows that are intended to work across frameworks. The post focuses on trust and control for agent development rather than a single framework-specific feature set.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog
